methods.description: Process for collecting and identifying 'Gazella' specimens from the Qingyang area, including the use of specific terminologies and measurements for classification.

steps.description: The 'Gazella' material at the Qingyang area was collected by E. Licent and is kept at Musée Hoangho Paiho.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect 'Gazella' specimens from Qingyang area and store at Musée Hoangho Paiho

steps.description: Terminology and nomenclature of the skull and teeth follow Gentry (1992), Dong (2004), and Sisson (1914).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply standard terminology and nomenclature to classify skull and teeth

steps.description: Measurements follow Bohlin (1939) and Suraprasit et al. (2015) and were taken with digital calipers with a precision of 0.1 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure specimens using digital calipers with 0.1 mm precision

methods.description: Classification of 'Gazella' specimens and proposal of a neotype due to the loss of the original type series.

steps.description: Considering that the comparable type series have been lost, we therefore propose a neotype.
steps.searchable_summary: Propose a neotype due to loss of original type series

steps.description: Neotype. M 3956, a nearly complete cranium without horn cores, housed at Uppsala Universitet Evolutionsmuseet.
